summary: Implemented the protected Radar Task UI slice on top of the existing local admin gate and existing Radar Task/Run Scan boundaries. The slice adds list, create, detail, edit, deactivate, recent run/latest opportunity context, and a client-side mock Run Scan control that calls the existing /api/scans/run endpoint with useMockSerp=true.

tests_run.result: PASS: 4 focused Radar Task view-model/form/run-scan tests passed.
PASS: Next.js production build passed; known local Windows SWC optional-native warnings were nonfatal.
PASS: focused local release smoke passed.
PASS: no whitespace errors; LF-to-CRLF warnings only.

decisions: Used server actions for create/update/deactivate so the UI does not expand or depend on unauthenticated CRUD routes.
Kept /api/scans/run as the only client-side request and forced useMockSerp=true through a small client-safe helper.
Split client-safe run-scan helpers from server-side Radar Task view-model loaders to avoid bundling Prisma/server loaders into the client component.
Kept delete behavior as explicit deactivation with confirmation copy, matching the existing soft-delete API.
Documented the local-only Radar Task UI and kept provider/deployment/ownership scope human-gated.
risks: The mock Run Scan control requires an authenticated session because /api/scans/run is protected by the local admin middleware.
The UI uses server actions and local APIs for self-use; production multi-user ownership remains out of scope.
Windows Next build still prints known nonfatal optional SWC warnings.
non_goal_confirmation: No production auth, multi-user ownership, real provider setup, paid service, public deployment, CI/CD, DNS, cron, email, billing, telemetry, package dependency, package-lock, Prisma schema/migration, scoring, LLM, SERP provider, or MVP Spec behavior change was added.
requested_action: Review UI/server-action boundaries, client/server imports, existing API contract usage, and task acceptance criteria before QA.
